expressions.english: eye disease
big eyes
expressions.notes:
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud
큰 is the adjective form of 크다.

expressions.english: to open one's eyes
expressions.notes: 눈을 뜨다 = to open one's eyes; to wake up

expressions.english: to close one's eyes
expressions.notes: 눈을 감다 = to close one's eyes

expressions.english: to be blinding, dazzling
expressions.notes: 눈이 부시다 = to be blinding, dazzling

expressions.english: I caught an eye disease.
expressions.notes:
걸리다 = to be caught; to catch (a disease)
병에 걸리다 = to get sick

expressions.english: That person has big eyes.
Tears started welling up in my eyes.
expressions.notes:
눈물 = tears
나다 = to come out
눈물이 나다 = tears flow; tears well up

expressions.english: She has beautiful eyes.
Open your eyes wide and look (at it closely).
expressions.notes: 크게 is an adverb form of 크다.

expressions.english: I think something got in my eye.
I cried all night long, so my eyes are swollen.
expressions.notes:
밤새 = overnight; all night long
울다 = to cry
-더니 = S + V-ed and as a result
붓다 = to swell

expressions.english: I am so sleepy that my eyes keep closing.
expressions.notes:
졸리다 = to be sleepy
자꾸 = repeatedly, again and again
눈이 감기다 = eyes close

expressions.english: Close your eyes. I have a present for you.
I turned a blind eye to my friend's lie.
expressions.notes:
거짓말 = lie
눈 감아 주다 = to turn a blind eye to something; to pretend not to have seen something happen

expressions.english: The sun is so bright that it's blinding.
expressions.notes:
햇살 = sunshine
강하다 = to be strong

expressions.english: I woke up early this morning.
expressions.notes:
오찍 = early; soon
눈이 떨어지다 = to wake up (without trying to)

expressions.korean: 눈이 따가울 때는 어떻게 해야 돼요?
expressions.english: When my eyes sting, what should I do?
As soon as I woke up this morning, I drank coffee.
expressions.notes:
-자마자 = as soon as + S + V
커피 = coffee
마시다 = to drink

expressions.korean: 눈이 잘 안 보여서 안경을 쓰기 시작했어요.
expressions.english: I couldn't see things clearly, so I started wearing glasses.
expressions.notes:
보이다 = to be seen; to be able to see
눈이 잘 안 보이다 = to be unable to see things well
안경 = glasses
쓰다 = to wear (eyewear, hat)
-기 시작하다 = to start + V-ing
